summary:
Blood transfusions of packed RBC must be administered within four hours after retrieval from the blood bank to ensure safety and efficacy. Strict adherence to specific time windows for starting or completing a transfusion beyond this timeframe is not clinically recommended based on current guidelines.

Blood transfusions of packed RBC should be completed within four hours after retrieval from the blood bank. Adhering strictly to fixed start times or exceeding these timelines outside standard protocol is inconsistent with accepted clinical practice.  

Final summary:

The transfusion of packed RBC must be initiated promptly upon delivery and completed within four hours post-retrieval. Clinical standards emphasize timely administration without rigidly enforcing exact start-times or extending past this window.
